1.11: With this in mind, we pray for you constantly—asking that our God will count you worthy of the calling you received, and that his power will bring to fulfillment every good intention and every act of faith.
1.3: We must always thank God for you, and rightly so—because your faith is growing more and more, and the love you have for each other keeps increasing.
1.7: And he will give relief to you who are suffering—and to us as well. This will happen when the Lord Jesus is revealed from heaven with his powerful angels,
1.10: on the day he comes to receive glory from his holy people and to be admired by all who have believed—and this includes you, because you believed what we told you.
1.9: They will be punished with eternal destruction—completely cut off from the presence of the Lord and from the glory of his power—
1.4: That's why we boast about you to other churches—we tell them about the endurance and faith you show in all the persecutions and suffering you are going through.
1.6: God's justice will ensure that those who are making you suffer will themselves suffer.
1.2: May God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give you grace and peace.
1.12: We want this so that the name of our Lord Jesus may be glorified in you and you in him—all by the grace of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ.
1.5: All of this is a clear sign that God's judgment is fair. Because you are suffering, God considers you worthy of his kingdom—for which you are enduring all this.
1.8: bringing judgment in blazing fire on those who don't know God and who refuse to obey the gospel of our Lord Jesus.
1.1: From Paul, Silas, and Timothy—to the church of the Thessalonians, who belong to God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

2.16: May our Lord Jesus Christ himself and God our Father—who loved us and by his grace gave us eternal encouragement and good hope—
2.17: encourage your hearts and strengthen you in every good deed and word.
2.13: But we must always thank God for you, brothers and sisters—you are loved by the Lord. God chose you from the beginning for salvation through the sanctifying work of the Spirit and through your belief in the truth.
2.10: He will use every kind of evil deception on those who are perishing—because they refused to love the truth that could have saved them.
2.1: Now, brothers and sisters, about the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and our being gathered together to him—
2.15: So then, stand firm, brothers and sisters, and hold tightly to the teaching we passed on to you—in person or by letter.
2.12: so that all who refused to believe the truth and instead chose wickedness will be condemned.
2.3: Don't let anyone fool you in any way. That day will not come until there is first a great rebellion against God, and the man of lawlessness—destined for destruction—is revealed.
2.8: Then the lawless one will be revealed—and the Lord Jesus will destroy him with the breath of his mouth, eliminating him by the blazing splendor of his coming.
2.7: The secret power of lawlessness is already at work—but the one who is holding it back will continue to do so until he is removed.
2.4: He will oppose and exalt himself above every so-called god and every object of worship. He will actually sit in the temple of God and declare himself to be God.
2.2: please don't be easily alarmed or suddenly upset—whether by a prophecy or a message or a letter claiming to be from us—saying that the day of the Lord has already arrived.
2.6: And you know what is currently holding him back—so that he will only be revealed at the right moment.
2.11: For this reason God allows a powerful delusion to overtake them, so they believe what is false—
2.14: He called you to this through the gospel we proclaimed, so you could share in the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
2.9: The lawless one's arrival will be accompanied by all the power of Satan, with counterfeit miracles and signs and wonders.
2.5: Don't you remember that I told you all this when I was with you?

3.5: May the Lord lead your hearts into a full understanding of God's love and the patient endurance that comes from Christ.
3.15: But don't treat them as an enemy—correct them as a fellow believer.
3.1: Brothers and sisters, please pray for us—that the Lord's message will spread quickly and be honored wherever it goes, just as it was with you.
3.13: As for the rest of you—never grow tired of doing what is right.
3.6: In the name of the Lord Jesus Christ, we command you, brothers and sisters: stay away from any believer who refuses to work and ignores the instructions we gave.
3.18: May the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all.
3.11: We've heard that some of you are not working at all—just meddling in other people's business.
3.8: we didn't eat anyone's food without paying for it. We worked hard day and night so we wouldn't be a burden to any of you.
3.9: We had the right to ask for support, but we worked instead—to give you an example to follow.
3.2: And pray that we will be rescued from wicked and evil people—not everyone shares our faith.
3.3: But the Lord is faithful. He will strengthen you and protect you from the evil one.
3.12: We command and urge such people, in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ, to settle down and earn their own living.
3.17: I, Paul, write this greeting in my own handwriting—this is my authenticating mark in every letter I write.
3.7: You know very well that you should follow our example. We were not idle when we were with you—
3.14: If anyone refuses to follow the instructions in this letter, take note of them. Don't associate with them—let them feel ashamed of their behavior.
3.16: May the Lord of peace himself give you his peace all the time in every situation. The Lord be with you all.
3.10: Even when we were with you, we gave you this rule: 'If anyone refuses to work, they shouldn't eat either.'
3.4: We have confidence in the Lord that you are doing and will keep doing what we tell you.
